title: Batch Visual Requests
description: The batch input consumed by `score_visual_items.py --batch-request`, written to <run>/analysis/visual-requests.json. THIS is the artifact a user edits and re-submits to make a per-slide component selection (component_id / allow_component_reuse / unresolved_policy). Slide selections live here — NOT in job-requirements.json. NOTE ON ENFORCEMENT: nothing evaluates this file as JSON Schema at runtime (`jsonschema` is not a declared dependency of this repo). score_visual_items.validate_batch_request() re-states these rules in code and runs before any scoring; test_gates.test_batch_request_validator_matches_schema_field_by_field reads THIS file and proves the code enforces every field and type it declares, so the two cannot drift apart silently. The code additionally rejects duplicate request_id and blank-only strings, which JSON Schema cannot express cleanly here.

brief.description: Optional plain-language note about the deck.

concepts.description: Optional required semantic concept groups: a list of groups, each a non-empty list of OR-alternative concept terms; the groups are AND requirements. When present, semantic coverage = matched_groups/total_groups and intent/tags feed retrieval/ranking only (they no longer inflate the required denominator). Omit for the legacy flat intent+tags behaviour.

content_shape.description: Required under --strict-shape; must be a shape in _common.SHAPE_TYPE_MAP.

component_id.description: EXPLICIT user selection: a stable published id (sun.component.x) OR the catalog 'Copy prompt' text, which is resolved deterministically to the id in parentheses. Bypasses the auto-confidence bar but is still validated (published + shape + slots) and fidelity-gated.

allow_component_reuse.description: Override the per-deck no-duplicate rule for THIS slide. Recorded in the decision and surfaced in review.

unresolved_policy.description: How the user explicitly resolves an unresolved (needs_component) slide without picking a component_id: "custom-local" authorises a custom slide, "blank" leaves it deliberately blank. Both are user-only outcomes. Omit to leave the slide unresolved (needs_component) — an unresolved job is NOT a deliverable.
